linkedin_en:
Imagine handing a search query to a helpful assistant, only to realize they treat your question as a direct command. That subtle shift is exactly how web breaches begin. The Busqueda machine on HackTheBox simulates a realistic web environment where outdated components and minor misconfigurations quietly hand over control to a patient attacker.

The breakthrough came from Python code injection via eval() in Searchor 2.4.0. When a web app uses eval(), it runs raw user text as executable Python instead of treating it as safe input, turning a simple text field into a full system backdoor whenever input is poorly sanitized. 

What stood out was how root access never required a single silver bullet, just a quiet chain of trusted processes executing flawed logic. Whether you architect infrastructure, write code, or simply expect your everyday apps to stay secure, this exercise proves that resilience relies on continuous patching, strict data validation, and healthy skepticism. Digital systems only weaken when we stop questioning what they are allowed to trust.

🌐 https://cyberstefan.nl
